assertions.title: Missing discovery endpoint opens setup instead of posting

steps.observed: Dashboard loaded with 78 jobs from the public sheet

steps.expected: Button enabled (no endpoint configured), clicking opens setup wizard
steps.observed: Button is DISABLED with title 'Finish the local discovery path in Settings, or click here to continue setup.'

steps.action: Attempt localStorage override with empty discovery transport setup
steps.observed: Override did not work - local bootstrap file is still being read and detected

steps.action: Attempt simulation override to disable local setup detection
steps.expected: Simulation override clears local setup detection
steps.observed: Simulation override did not work - local bootstrap file still detected

assertions.issues: The local bootstrap file (discovery-local-bootstrap.json) exists in the repo and contains localWebhookUrl, causing localSetupDetected to be true. This makes the Run discovery button disabled with a different UI state than the assertion expects. The assertion assumes no local bootstrap is present, but the test environment has one.

assertions.title: Managed Apps Script stays marked as stub-only

steps.action: Open Settings modal and navigate to Discovery tab
steps.observed: Discovery tab opened successfully after using JavaScript to click (normal click blocked by onboarding dialog)

steps.action: Enter fake Apps Script stub URL: https://script.google.com/macros/s/AKCf5bwvJbO8xCxMvSfCfJh2GqJxQwE8eQvLkPpDqRrSsTt/exec

steps.expected: Test triggers CORS failure, classified as stub_only with warning message
steps.observed: Test webhook clicked, but no visible result. Fake URL returns 404 instead of CORS failure, so stub_only classification not triggered.

steps.action: Check for managed Apps Script deployment
steps.expected: Managed Apps Script URL available for testing
steps.observed: No managed Apps Script deployed (getManagedAppsScriptWebhookIdentity returns empty string)

evidence.network: no captured requests (agent-browser did not capture the POST)

assertions.issues: The assertion requires testing with a real Apps Script endpoint that triggers CORS failure. A fake URL returns 404, which doesn't trigger the handleAppsScriptBrowserCorsFailure path. No managed Apps Script is deployed in the test environment, so the stub_only classification cannot be properly tested.

frictions.description: Onboarding Welcome dialog appears on every page load, blocking UI interaction
frictions.resolution: Used Escape key to close the dialog, or used JavaScript to directly interact with elements

frictions.description: agent-browser click commands fail with 'element blocked by another element' even after closing dialogs
frictions.resolution: Used JavaScript eval to directly click elements via DOM methods

frictions.description: Network requests not captured by agent-browser network tracking
frictions.resolution: Could not resolve - the POST requests from the webhook test were not captured

blockers.description: Local bootstrap file (discovery-local-bootstrap.json) interferes with VAL-DISCOVERY-001 test
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried localStorage overrides and simulation overrides, but the bootstrap file is fetched on every snapshot build and its data takes precedence

blockers.description: No real Apps Script endpoint available for VAL-DISCOVERY-004 test
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried fake URL, but it returns 404 instead of triggering CORS failure. The stub_only classification requires a real Apps Script URL that triggers CORS.

summary: Tested 2 assertions: both blocked. VAL-DISCOVERY-001 blocked by local bootstrap file interference - the file exists and causes localSetupDetected=true, making the Run discovery button disabled instead of enabled. VAL-DISCOVERY-004 blocked by lack of real Apps Script endpoint - a fake URL returns 404 instead of CORS failure, preventing the stub_only classification from being triggered.
